title: Create a new event subscriptions
description: An event subscription allows for you to request a subscription and the API will post data back to your requested url. This is usefully for server side implementations needing near real time updates. Multiple "models" are available like subscriber,presence,call, auditlog.
name: Create

Parameter.description:
The model of the subscription you wish to monitor. Options include...
"call" - object that will send active call updates. Expect a unique data set per term callid and term device. A json field "remove" set to "yes" will be added when the call is completed.
"call_origid" - similar to "call", but its unique entry per orig_callid. This will generate less data on things like sim ring and transfers.
"subscriber" - updates to the user objects for the requested data.
"presence" - similar to "subscriber" but only updates on presence and status message updates. Available for more scopes.
"auditlog" - will stream any changes to the audit log, adds, updates and deletes for all objects.
"message" - used for Chat and SMS applications, will stream posts on new message requests.
"agent" - will update when status of call center agents change.
A full url in either http or https format where new data will be sent to. Must be publicly accessible.
Optional expires time in standard myql datetime format to allow for extended subscriptions. Default expires time is the expires time of the oauth_token.
The domain to limit the subscription for. Needed for Reseller or lower scopes.

Success 200.description: A unique ID corresponding to the new subscription. Save and use for any updates or deletes of the subscription.

description: When updating a certain time_range this will take a lot of logic into play, which a lot of it is going to be in the portal timeframes_controller. When updating a timeframe the portal will look and see whether any timeranges will need to be created. It will then create that timeframe and place it in the correct order. This might be confusing when a user tries to update a TimeFrame by deleting a timeRange and also adding another timeRange at the same time and the ordering may be off by 1 since the old timeRange objects will still contain their old ordering and not be reindexed, only the newly created timeRanges will contain reindexed 'order' fields.

description: This is the creation of a TimeRange, which is a subsequent object within TimeFrames. The timerange is the actual object that we see carry the data for the timing rules and when to start/stop a certain task, may it be answering rules or etc. There are multiple timeRanges per timeFrames if the ranges span over a timeperiod that cannot be contained to a single range. An example a time that could be contained to a single range is say "From 2018-02-26 14:00 - 2018-02-26 15:00". What makes this able to be in one timeRange is that it only spans one day and all the time has no gaps in it. What is different about timeRanges is that since there may be multiple timeRanges for a timeFrame, there needs to be an order in which they are shown. This is done by using the parameter 'order'. This will determine how the user will see this timeRange when editing the timeFrame.
